identity.name: Antler Kitchen & Bar

identity.location: 1454 Dundas St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1Y6, Canada

narrative.summary: You're on West Dundas in a wood-heavy room with wide windows, exposed brick, and a chef's table where your server might also be the one plating your venison. Chef Alex runs a tight operation focused on Canadian game—wild boar, house-cured elk, whatever's been hunted that week—all treated with French technique and plated like they matter. The tasting menu runs $85-95 per person, and if you spring for the chef's table, Alex himself walks you through each course.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on a stretchy section of West Dundas, where the restaurant sits with wide windows that flood the wood-heavy room with natural light during the day. Inside, exposed brick meets dark timber, and the chef's table gives you a straight sightline into the kitchen where your server might also be the one plating your venison. It's intimate without feeling cramped—maybe forty seats total, with that lived-in warmth of a room where regulars actually exist.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Chef Alex runs a tight operation focused on Canadian game and foraged ingredients, the kind of cooking that namedrops the wilderness without feeling precious about it. You'll see dishes built around wild boar, house-cured elk, and whatever's been hunted or gathered that week, all treated with French technique and plated like they matter. The tasting menu runs around $85-95 per person, and if you spring for the chef's table, expect Alex himself to walk you through each course.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Antler Kitchen & Bar totally blew me away for my birthday dinner. We got the chef’s table with the tasting menu, and from start to finish, everything was beautifully made and super flavorful.

Chef Alex and his crew made the night feel really special. He personally served us all evening, explained each dish with so much passion, and you could tell how much thought went into every little thing. The whole team was friendly, professional, and super attentive without ever being in the way.

The food really highlighted tasty Canadian ingredients and wild-inspired dishes in such a fancy, creative way, and the timing of the tasting menu was spot on. The cocktails were amazing too – balanced, creative, and a perfect match for the food.

The cozy, rustic vibe, open kitchen, and chill atmosphere made it a perfect spot for a special occasion, but it still felt comfy and down-to-earth. Honestly, it was one of the best dining experiences I’ve had in Toronto, and I’m so thankful to Chef Alex and the team for making my birthday so memorable. I’d totally recommend Antler to anyone looking for a unique and unforgettable night out."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Finally got a chance to dine at Antler with their Chef's Table Tasting Menu. Came with high expectations and what we got exceeded it. Each of their dishes were spot on, but the highlights for us were the Venison Tartare topped with an awesome fried shiitake, their smoked boar belly and the bison steak with a duck fat potato rosti. Value for the price, we would happily come back for it again."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We love Antler so much! Having been loyal customers for a long time, when we accepted a job in Australia, leaving Antler was on the “con” list…so we were very happy to squeeze in a dinner on our first visit back to Canada last month.
As always the meal, service and atmosphere were really special. Although restaurants in Melbourne are great, there is only one Antler. Don’t miss it."
